Description:
Ballistics of the F-56 time-fuzed fragmentation shell fired from the 100mm B-34-U100 gun, derived from the earlier B-14 prototype. The gun was employed in the PRC with locally produced ammunition.

Soviet ammunition includes the ZS-56 time-delay airbursting fragmentation and ZS-56P radio proximity fuzed airbursting fragmentation rounds, the F-56 HE round, the SB-56 star shell, and the PB-56 anti-radar shell.

Chinese ammunition includes the WB709P high-explosive round, analogous to the F-56, weighs 15.8 kg and loads 5.5 kg of a mix of 22/1-23 and 12/1-29 propellant grains, develops 3000 kgf/cm^2 and 895 m/s from a 7.84 L (7.2dm long) chamber and 4656 mm of projectile travel. The WB700P air-burst fragmentation round, analogous to the ZS-56 shell, weighs 15.6 kg and develops 900 m/s. Domestically developed auxiliary rounds includes the WB415P illumination round and the WB442 incendiary round.

The ballistics of the 100x695mm caliber (D-10, KS-19, BS-3) is derived from that of the B-34.

References:
 - 《火炮内弹道计算手册》王连荣，张佩勤，国防工业出版社，1987
